Text:
A significant pay raise is coming for Zimbabwe's lowest-paid mine workers!

Starting soon, the least paid in the mining sector will see their monthly earnings increase to US$418 as a new wage agreement takes effect. This is a crucial development for thousands of families and a step towards improving livelihoods in one of the country's most vital industries.

For many, this agreement means a tangible difference in their daily lives, offering greater financial stability and a glimmer of hope for a brighter future. It's a reminder of the ongoing efforts to ensure fair compensation within Zimbabwe's workforce.

Text:
Tired of information overload? We've got you covered.

Navigating the vast ocean of global news to find what truly impacts Zimbabwe can feel like a full-time job. You're juggling The Guardian, Reuters, NYT, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, and even the Herald, trying to piece together the real story. Who has the time?

We do. The Mutapa Times editorial team sifts through 30+ international and local outlets every week. We meticulously filter out the noise, delivering only the 10-15 most critical stories that genuinely matter for Zimbabwe, straight to your inbox. Get the clarity and context you need, without the endless scrolling.

Tap the link in our bio to subscribe — it's free.

Text:
Feeling a little piece of home missing? 🇿🇼

For every Zimbabwean abroad – whether you're braving the London chill, chasing the American dream, navigating Jo'burg's hustle, or enjoying Aussie sunshine – we know that pull. That longing to hear the latest from Harare, to feel the pulse of Bulawayo, to understand the economy that shapes your family's future, or even just to know how the Sables are doing or what's topping the Zim music charts.

The Mutapa Times Briefing is your direct link. It's more than news; it's your connection to the rhythm of Zimbabwe, bridging the miles so you never feel truly far from home. Stay informed, stay connected, stay Zimbabwean, wherever you are in the world.
